titleStmt.title: SII 17.643: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 643. (A.R. No. 593 of 1904.) ŚRĪNIVĀSANALLŪR, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south wall of the Kuraṅganātha temple.
This inscription is dated in the reign of Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarman. A transaction made in the 19th year is also mentioned.

It records a sale of land made free of the taxes, iṟai, echchōṟu, veṭṭi vedaṉai, to Tirukkurakkuttuṟaip-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ, by the mūlaparaḍai of Mahēndramaṅgalam, a brahmadēya receiving gold worth 233 kaḻañju and 5(1/2) mā, this amount being probably the dues on account of the pañchavāranelluFor the exact significance of this term see. Ep. Ind., Vol. XXIII pp. 23-24.to Kuṟumbiluḍaiyār and Nāraṇamaṅgalamuḍaiyār, by Gāṉgayattu Śōḻakōṉār.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
matirai koṇṭa kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu 2 10 Ā(vatu) piramateya mayentiramaṅka lattu mulapa
raṭaiyom tirukkurakkuttuṟaip perumāṉaṭikaḷukku muṉ kaiyyeḻuttolaiyāṟ kaṭava poṉ 100 7 10 5 �ndha2 yāṇṭu 10 9 Āvatu kuṟumpiluṭaiyārkkum nāraṇamaṅkalamuṭaiyārk kuṅ kaṭamai pañcavāra nellu kā
ṉkayattu coḻakoṉār tara niṉṟa taṇṭukkup poka Oṭattaṭṭiṉa nāṉūṟṟu kalattāl 10 2 vaṇṇattāl poṉ Āka pācciṟ kallāṟ ṟuḷaippoṉ Irunūṟṟu muppattu mukkaḻañcey Aimmāvarai
ppoṉṉukku māppattu vaṇṇamāka vanta nilam 1�ma Oṉṟe muṉṟumākkāṇinilam viṟṟukkuṭuttaṟ kicaitta Ellai paḷḷiccantattukku kiḻakkum kuṇṭai vaḷḷi vāy kālukku teṟku maṇaliṭu nikki peruvaḻikku
vaṭakkum Eṅkaḷ potu nilattukku meṟkum Ivvicaitta perunāṉkellaiyi lakappaṭṭa nilam Oṉṟe muṉṟu mākkāṇi nilattāl vanta Iṟaiyum Eccoṟum veṭṭivetaṉai Epperppaṭṭatuṅ kāttuk
kuṭuppatākavum Innilañcuṭṭi cennirppotuviṉai ceyyātatākavum tevatāṉamāka viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu kuṭuttom mulaparaṭaiyom tirukkurakkuttuṟai perumāṉaṭi kaḷukku .
Digital edition of SII 17.643 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
